about: Authoritative success-envelope contract for the DEV-only mobile Claw VPN owner-present ceremony.
This fixture defines bytes and boundary metadata only. It installs no route, handler, runtime, RP, adapter, or effect path.
The separate owner_present_error_wire_v1.json contract must be landed and pinned in both repositories before any handler or client may implement these paths.
This V1 file is immutable after both repositories land it; incompatible changes require a new versioned fixture.

format.canonical_cbor: RFC 8949 deterministic encoding; every envelope is decoded, re-encoded, and required to be byte-identical.
format.challenge_id: Exactly 16 server-random bytes encoded as 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters. It is the only public challenge handle.
format.proof_token: Exactly 32 opaque random bytes encoded as a CBOR byte string. It is adapter-only, never enters summary, UI, debug, or logs, and is not a RevalidatedCapability.
format.run_claims: Caller-supplied DEV correlation claims. StartResponse copies claw_alias and run_claims byte-for-byte from its explicitly referenced StartRequest into a server-owned execution template, resolves claw_id only through server_selector_bindings, recomputes the execution hash/context, and the client must require exact equality before the owner gesture. They never authorize member, household, owner, device, Claw, config, enrollment, ACL, audience, bundle, times, or nonces. If a trusted readiness provider lands later, any mismatch must burn rather than fall back to these claims.
format.owner_credential_allowlist: options.publicKey.allowCredentials is nonempty and derived exclusively from the owner's server-side enrollment for the trusted RP; no request field may influence it.
format.webauthn_binding: options.publicKey.challenge is a random RP challenge and is not context.challengeDigest. The server binds challenge_id one-to-one to the stored context, consumes it at finish, and requires stored, submitted, and reconstructed contexts to be byte-identical.
format.error_contract: Out of scope. Authority-sensitive failures must eventually share one opaque, indistinguishable form; no handler or client may land until owner_present_error_wire_v1.json is authoritative and cross-repo pinned.
